ogTitle: Daveyston vs Davoren Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Daveyston and Davoren Park.
heading: Daveyston vs Davoren Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Daveyston, South Australia and Davoren Park, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Davoren Park has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Daveyston (31/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Daveyston or Davoren Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Daveyston has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Davoren Park scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Daveyston scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Davoren Park scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Daveyston or Davoren Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Davoren Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.08% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
